name: Simple Veal Piccata
recipeInstructions:
1. Season flour with salt and pepper.
2. Dredge the veal cutlets in seasoned flour, coating them evenly on all sides.
3. Heat a heavy-bottomed skillet over medium heat until it's hot but not smoking.
4. Add butter to the skillet and let it melt, allowing foam to subside first.
5. Place the dredged cutlets into the skillet and sauté for 1-2 minutes per side, depending on thickness, ensuring they are lightly browned without overcooking.
6. Remove the veal cutlets from the pan and keep them warm.
7. In the same skillet, add another tablespoon of butter and reduce heat to medium-low.
8. Squeeze the juice of one lemon into the skillet and stir until it reduces slightly.
9. Season the sauce with salt and pepper to taste, if needed.
10. Return the veal cutlets to the pan to baste them with the sauce, cooking for an additional 30 seconds to a minute on each side.
11. Sprinkle minced parsley over the top of the cooked veal before serving.
PETER KUMP - PRODIGY GUEST CHEFS COOKBOOK
